content:
Long press one or more messages to select them, then press the dustbin icon at the top of the screen to delete.

You can also setup a left or right swipe action to delete a message. It's configured from settings > general settings > interaction > swipe actions

If the dustbin icon is not displayed when viewing an email, navigate to ... Settings > general settings > display > visible message actions Tick the delete action's checkbox.
creator: platform34

content:
Check that notifications and sync are enabled on the applicable folder.

  1. Select your account on the side bar (folders displayed)
  2. Tap manage folders (bottom of list)
  3. Tap your folders name
  4. Toggle 'Nofifications' ON
  5. Toggle 'Enable sync' ON
creator: platform34

content:
Thunderbird for Android doesn't support the use of oAuth 2.0 with Pop connections. I suspect your desktop accounts are setup with this configuration and imported by QR code into the mobile app. This would explain the error you see.

If this is the case, you have two options.

  1. Continue using oAuth 2.0 authentication. You must change from Pop3 to an Imap connection.
  2. Continue using a Pop connection. You must ..

a. Generate an app password via your providers webmail settings. b. Change authentication type in TBA accounts from oAuth 2.0 to normal user name & password. c. Enter the newly generated app password in the password field.

Note: App passwords are a generated 16 digit passcode, not your normal email account password.

To check if Imap or pop is being used ... Navigate to settings > account name > fetching mail > incoming server.

Pop Server name = pop.gmail.com Port = 995

Imap Server name = imap.gmail.com Port = 993

Imap is the modern connection protocol, supporting sync on multiple devices.
creator: platform34
